title: RuVector embedding and semantic database editing defined
objective: Define embedding generation, hybrid retrieval, semantic SQL edits, content-addressed code blocks, copy-on-write branches, temporal alternatives, MinCut isolation, witness chains, and deterministic merge selection inside PostgreSQL plus RuVector, assigning every semantic-edit entity exactly once with owner, lifetime, sync edge, and recovery under the STORE-001 authority model, without computing embeddings, writing vectors, merging branches, or overwriting source.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (store) / envctl (compute) / committed model pin (provenance)
work_items.decision: local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim, Candle/ORT) computes vectors during the envctl bridge and commits them to PostgreSQL; the database only stores and measures. Every vector is bound to an embedding_model_pin (model id, weights_sha256, dim, metric) so re-embedding reproduces the identical vector. redb serves the BLAKE3 dedup cache as a transient tier.

retrieval is standard SQL combining a relational predicate (language, status) with vector nearest-neighbour (ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> [target] LIMIT k), BM25 keyword search, and FastGRNN pre-filter, executed in-database; redb performs f32 distance math only as a cache with no SQL/secondary index/native vector logic.

work_items.surface: content-addressed code blocks + semantic SQL edits
work_items.decision: code blocks are SHA-256 content-addressed (ADOPT-001 BlobStore contract) with raw_code, AST fragment, module_path, and causal edges; edits are standard SQL UPDATEs applied only inside COW branch rows, producing a new content hash, re-embedding, and refreshed causal edges. No main-trunk-direct edit and no source-file overwrite.

concurrent edits are isolated in MVCC COW staging branches cloned from the main trunk; ATAS/ESN simulate parallel timelines (state diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, failure nodes) up to 100 steps and score each by mathematical stability (cosine similarity, strange-attractor bounds); timelines are transient/derived and never durable authority.

work_items.surface: MinCut isolation + witness chains + causal verification
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (integrity) / AgentDB (per-agent witness facts, rooted in PostgreSQL)
work_items.decision: RuVector MinCut (O(n^{o(1)})) severs hallucinating agent clusters in real time; every claim/edit must carry a SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector and pass Causal Graph Verification (a historical causal edge must exist), or it is rejected before it can merge.

work_items.surface: deterministic merge gates + rollback + source reconstruction
work_items.decision: merge gate = tests pass AND witness rooted AND MinCut/causal integrity intact AND ESN-selected winning timeline; the merge commits already-computed winning branch rows to the trunk (the model is never asked to redo the work). Failed branches roll back; PostgreSQL PITR + redb application-level WAL recover state; envctl re-projects byte-exact source from module_path. Autonomous merge is blocked in this planning cell; a later executor performs a gated, non-autonomous commit.

completion_gate: generated/ruvector_semantic_edit_plan.json assigns every semantic-edit entity exactly once with owner, lifetime, sync edge, recovery, and PostgreSQL/redb/CodeDB relationship; all ten verification-gate clauses (model+vector provenance, reproducible embedding, hybrid search, branch isolation, concurrent edits, temporal scoring, witness-bound decisions, deterministic merge gates, rollback, source reconstruction) map to concrete contracts grounded in the Architecture Blueprint and STORE-001; POSTGRES-005.proof.json records zero product execution (no embeddings, no writes, no merge, no source overwrite).
grounded_in: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
